articles.title: Elon Musk’s worst enemy in court is Elon Musk
articles.summary: Elon Musk's courtroom testimony proved problematic as he struggled during cross-examination, refusing direct answers and contradicting earlier statements, undermining his own case.

articles.title: Google Search queries hit an ‘all time high’ last quarter
articles.summary: Google Search queries reached an all-time high in Q1 2026, with AI experiences driving increased usage and 19% revenue growth. CEO Sundar Pichai credits AI investments and full-stack approach for strong performance across the business.

articles.title: All the evidence unveiled so far in Musk v. Altman
articles.summary: The Musk v. Altman trial reveals evidence including emails and documents from OpenAI's founding. Key findings show Musk drafted OpenAI's mission, Nvidia provided computing resources, and early concerns existed about Musk's involvement.

articles.title: SoftBank is creating a robotics company that builds data centers — and already eyeing a $100B IPO
articles.summary: SoftBank is launching a new robotics company to construct data centers, leveraging AI and robots to build AI infrastructure. The venture already targets a $100B IPO valuation.

articles.title: Amazon’s cloud business is surging — and so is its capital spending
articles.summary: Amazon's AWS cloud business is experiencing strong revenue growth, but the company is simultaneously increasing capital spending and plans to continue doing so in the near term.

articles.title: Sources: Anthropic could raise a new $50B round at a valuation of $900B
articles.summary: Anthropic, maker of Claude AI, has received multiple pre-emptive offers for a $50B funding round at valuations between $850B-$900B.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million Series B to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS. The funding capitalizes on developer demand for simpler, cost-effective alternatives to legacy cloud platforms.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges $20-$200 monthly for AI coding, but free alternative Goose offers similar functionality as open-source software running locally without subscription fees or limitations.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI-encoded tokens and a coding challenge that attracted thousands of applicants and helped recruit engineers.

articles.title: Why a recent supply-chain attack singled out security firms Checkmarx and Bitwarden
articles.summary: Security firm Checkmarx suffered multiple supply-chain attacks within six weeks, including a Trivy scanner compromise and direct GitHub account breach that delivered malware to customers seeking credentials.

articles.title: Open source package with 1 million monthly downloads stole user credentials
articles.summary: A popular open-source package with 1M monthly downloads was compromised, allowing attackers to steal user credentials including API tokens, SSH keys, and cloud provider credentials through a malicious release.

articles.title: Why are top university websites serving porn? It comes down to shoddy housekeeping.
articles.summary: Prestigious university websites including Berkeley, Columbia, and Washington University are serving pornographic and malicious content through subdomains due to poor administrative record-keeping. Researcher found hundreds of compromised subdomains across at least 34 universities being exploited by scammers.

articles.title: The Download: storing nuclear waste and orchestrating agents
articles.summary: Nuclear energy gains political support and Big Tech investment, but the US lacks permanent storage for 2,000 metric tons of high-level waste produced annually, making an urgent solution necessary.

articles.title: It’s time to make a plan for nuclear waste
articles.summary: Nuclear energy is experiencing renewed interest from tech companies, but the US still lacks a long-term solution for managing 2,000 metric tons of high-level nuclear waste produced annually.

articles.title: The Download: Musk and Altman’s legal showdown, and AI’s profit problem
articles.summary: Elon Musk sues OpenAI's Sam Altman, seeking $134 billion in damages and the company's conversion back to non-profit status. The trial could significantly impact OpenAI's planned IPO and leadership.

articles.title: AI evals are becoming the new compute bottleneck
articles.summary: AI evaluation processes are emerging as a major computational constraint, rivaling training compute requirements. Organizations struggle with scaling evaluation infrastructure to assess model capabilities effectively.

articles.title: Granite 4.1 LLMs: How They’re Built
articles.summary: Granite 4.1 LLMs are built using advanced architectural improvements and training methodologies that enhance performance and efficiency. The models incorporate refined techniques for better language understanding and generation capabilities.

articles.title: DeepInfra on Hugging Face Inference Providers 🔥
articles.summary: DeepInfra joins Hugging Face's inference provider network, enabling developers to access AI models through integrated services. This partnership expands model deployment options for the machine learning community.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses future directions in AI development and innovation. The conversation explores upcoming trends and challenges in the rapidly evolving artificial intelligence landscape.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing. These AI solutions enable companies to streamline workflows and generate more imaginative campaigns efficiently.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural technology tools publicly available to advance sustainable farming practices and innovation.

articles.title: Celebrating 20 years of Google Translate: Fun facts, tips and new features to try
articles.summary: Google Translate celebrates 20 years of translation services, evolving from a 2006 AI experiment to supporting nearly 250 languages today with new features and fun facts.

articles.title: Join the new AI Agents Vibe Coding Course from Google and Kaggle
articles.summary: Google and Kaggle are offering a 5-Day AI Agents Intensive Course with registration now open. This vibe coding course provides hands-on training in AI agent development.

articles.title: 8 Gemini tips for organizing your space (and life)
articles.summary: Google Gemini offers AI-powered tips for organizing your physical and digital spaces, including cleaning schedules, inbox management, and seasonal chores.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with the Republic of Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using advanced AI models.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo introduces a new method for resilient distributed AI training that can tolerate failures and communication delays. It enables more efficient large-scale model training across multiple nodes.

articles.title: Partnering with industry leaders to accelerate AI transformation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with global consultancies to help organizations worldwide adopt frontier AI technology and accelerate their digital transformation initiatives.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast #242 discusses major AI news including OpenAI's improved ChatGPT image model for accurate text generation, Qwen 3.6 Max, and Kimi-K2.6 releases.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and higher per-token prices but improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launches DLSS 5, a generative AI technology that enhances video game graphics with real-time photorealism improvements. OpenAI develops a superapp while MiniMax releases M2.7 model.

articles.title: IDC: How EMEA CIOs can jumpstart AI rollouts
articles.summary: EMEA CIOs must audit systems to accelerate stalled AI rollouts. Only 9% of organizations achieved measurable business outcomes, with 91% facing execution and financial validation challenges.

articles.title: GPT-5.5 is OpenAI’s most capable agentic AI model yet
articles.summary: OpenAI launched GPT-5.5, its most capable agentic AI model, designed to plan, use tools, and complete tasks independently with minimal human intervention. The model is rolling out to ChatGPT and Codex users across all tiers.

articles.title: IBM launches AI platform Bob to regulate SDLC costs
articles.summary: IBM launches Bob, an AI platform designed to manage software development lifecycle costs and governance. The tool balances AI-driven development speed with necessary controls, compliance, and security requirements.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study reveals that AI coding assistant Claude Code inadvertently leaks sensitive API keys to public code repositories, posing security risks.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeover attacks, potentially creating a major supply chain vulnerability.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: LLM self-distillation optimizes for speed but reduces generalization ability. Training models for concise answers impairs their capacity to explore alternative solutions on complex problems.

articles.title: IBM Releases Two Granite Speech 4.1 2B Models: Autoregressive ASR with Translation and Non-Autoregressive Editing for Fast Inference
articles.summary: IBM released two compact 2B-parameter Granite Speech models for efficient automatic speech recognition and translation across six languages. The models balance computational efficiency with accuracy for enterprise production deployments.

articles.title: Cursor Introduces a TypeScript SDK for Building Programmatic Coding Agents With Sandboxed Cloud VMs, Subagents, Hooks, and Token-Based Pricing
articles.summary: Cursor launches a TypeScript SDK in public beta, enabling developers to programmatically access its AI coding agent technology. This shifts Cursor from an interactive IDE to deployable infrastructure that organizations can integrate into existing systems.

articles.title: Top 10 KV Cache Compression Techniques for LLM Inference: Reducing Memory Overhead Across Eviction, Quantization, and Low-Rank Methods
articles.summary: KV cache memory is a major bottleneck in LLM inference, consuming 72-180 GB for models. The article explores ten compression techniques including eviction and quantization to reduce memory overhead and improve throughput.

articles.title: Privacy in the AI era is possible, says Proton's CEO, but one thing keeps him up at night
articles.summary: Proton's CEO Andy Yen discusses privacy challenges in the AI era, emphasizing that privacy is achievable but mass surveillance remains a concern. He addresses protecting children and local AI while acknowledging limitations Proton faces.

articles.title: This simple Linux tweak fixes crashes automatically - and it costs me nothing
articles.summary: A Linux Watchdog feature automatically monitors system responsiveness and reboots when problems occur, providing a free solution to prevent crashes.

articles.title: Windows changes are coming: Here's how to get a sneak peek at what's next
articles.summary: Microsoft simplifies its Windows Insider Program with upcoming changes. Users can preview new features by joining the program to test upcoming Windows updates.

articles.title: Building AI Agents in Python with Pydantic AI
articles.summary: Pydantic AI enables developers to build AI agents in Python with structured data validation. The framework simplifies agent development by providing type-safe tools and seamless integration with language models.

articles.title: Effective Context Engineering for AI Agents: A Developer’s Guide
articles.summary: This guide teaches developers how to effectively engineer context for AI agents, optimizing their performance and accuracy through better prompt design and information structuring.

articles.title: Text Summarization with Scikit-LLM
articles.summary: Scikit-LLM enables text summarization by integrating large language models with scikit-learn's familiar API, simplifying NLP tasks for developers.

articles.title: 4 YAML Files Instead of PySpark: How We Let Analysts Build Data Pipelines Without Engineers
articles.summary: A company replaced Python-based PySpark pipelines with YAML configuration files using dlt, dbt, and Trino, enabling analysts to build data pipelines independently and reducing delivery time from weeks to one day.

articles.title: Ensembles of Ensembles of Ensembles: A Guide to Stacking
articles.summary: Combining multiple machine learning models through stacking ensemble techniques improves performance better than using a single model.

articles.title: Agentic AI: How to Save on Tokens
articles.summary: The article explores techniques to reduce token usage in agentic AI systems, including caching, lazy-loading, routing, and compaction strategies to optimize efficiency and costs.

articles.title: NVIDIA Launches Nemotron 3 Nano Omni Model, Unifying Vision, Audio and Language for up to 9x More Efficient AI Agents
articles.summary: NVIDIA launches Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, a unified multimodal AI model combining vision, audio, and language capabilities into a single system for 9x more efficient AI agents with improved reasoning and faster responses.

articles.title: Into the Omniverse: Manufacturing’s Simulation-First Era Has Arrived
articles.summary: Manufacturing is entering a simulation-first era where digital environments replace traditional real-world testing. NVIDIA Omniverse and OpenUSD enable developers to transform workflows through advanced virtual simulation.

articles.title: OpenAI’s New GPT-5.5 Powers Codex on NVIDIA Infrastructure — and NVIDIA Is Already Putting It to Work
articles.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 now powers Codex, an agentic coding application running on NVIDIA's GB200 infrastructure. Over 10,000 NVIDIA employees across departments report significant productivity gains using the system.

articles.title: Extracting contract insights with PwC’s AI-driven annotation on AWS
articles.summary: PwC developed an AI-driven annotation solution on AWS to extract structured insights from contracts, addressing scalability challenges traditional keyword-based methods face in legal and compliance analysis.

articles.title: Organizing Agents’ memory at scale: Namespace design patterns in AgentCore Memory
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Memory uses namespaces to organize, retrieve, and secure AI agent memory across sessions. Proper namespace design prevents irrelevant context retrieval and security vulnerabilities.

articles.title: Building AI-ready data: Vanguard’s Virtual Analyst journey
articles.summary: Vanguard developed a Virtual Analyst using conversational AI to help financial analysts query complex datasets without writing SQL, requiring robust AI-ready data infrastructure.

articles.title: Powering AI Factories with NVIDIA Enterprise Reference Architectures
articles.summary: NVIDIA introduces Enterprise Reference Architectures for AI factories, enabling organizations to deploy agentic AI systems at scale with reasoning and automation capabilities for competitive advantage.

articles.title: Scaling Biomolecular Modeling Using Context Parallelism in NVIDIA BioNeMo
articles.summary: NVIDIA BioNeMo uses context parallelism to overcome GPU memory limitations in biomolecular modeling, enabling researchers to study complete biological systems instead of isolated protein fragments.

articles.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni Powers Multimodal Agent Reasoning in a Single Efficient Open Model
articles.summary: NVIDIA releases Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, an efficient open model enabling multimodal agent reasoning across text, audio, video, and documents in a single unified system.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms, revealing generational differences in consumer attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint and sharing these solutions openly with others.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background pioneered breakthrough extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse perspectives drive innovation in cutting-edge tech development.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling long-horizon planning through trajectory lifting, stochastic exploration, and gradient reshaping to improve optimization in high-dimensional vision spaces.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers at Berkeley develop methods for identifying feature interactions in large language models at scale to improve interpretability and AI safety. The work analyzes how input features collectively drive model predictions.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on designing imaging systems using information-driven approaches. An encoder maps objects to images, which noise corrupts into measurements, with an information estimator quantifying measurement quality.
